sourceMovie.title: I Can Only Imagine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto I Can Only Imagine (2018) as a music theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The music and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: I Still Believe
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I Can Only Imagine clicked for you, I Still Believe (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in music and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Back to Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I Can Only Imagine clicked for you, Back to Black (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in music and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I Can Only Imagine?
faq.answer: Try I Still Believe, Back to Black, A Week Away—each shares DNA with I Can Only Imagine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I Can Only Imagine?
faq.answer: I Can Only Imagine (2018) is best described as Music, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I Can Only Imagine part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "I Can Only Imagine Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
